originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'flags' is never read

presenceCondition:
(or (and CROSS_COMPILE (not WIN32) NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES)
    (and CROSS_COMPILE WIN32 NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES)
    (and (not CROSS_COMPILE) WIN32 (not NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES))
    (and (not CROSS_COMPILE) (not WIN32) NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES)
    (and (not CROSS_COMPILE) WIN32 NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES))
model:
(define-fun WIN32 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CROSS_COMPILE () Bool
  false)
reasoning: c76df14dfb4.c L38: 'flags |= T_ESCAPE_SHELL_CMD' dead store inside #ifdef NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES. flags is assigned in the loop body but its value is discarded at each loop iteration (reset to 0 at L21) and never used outside the loop. The dead store at L38 exists only when NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES is defined. However, the PC includes '!CROSS_COMPILE AND WIN32 AND !NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES' — a config where L38 does not exist (it's inside #ifdef NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES). This over-constrains the PC by including non-compilable cases for L38. New bug.
